2605.22859.title: Staging by the Book: Automatic Sleep Stage Classification Using Scoring Rules
2605.22859.paper_summary: Automated sleep staging is commonly approached as a supervised machine learning problem, with deep learning methods dominating recent research. While machine learning models achieve near-human level agreement with human-scored reference sleep stages, their decisions are typically opaque and not designed to follow clinical scoring rules. We propose a transparent alternative: a deterministic, rule-based sleep staging method that explicitly operationalizes the American Academy of Sleep Medicine's (AASM) scoring logic as executable code, coupled with epoch-level natural-language justifications derived from an explanation trace. We evaluate the approach on 50 polysomnography recordings with a 10-scorer majority-vote consensus as reference. Across all recordings, the method agreed with the majority-vote reference in 60.5% of epochs ($κ=0.42$), with substantially higher agreement on a dataset used during development (77.1%, $κ=0.61$). Agreement with the reference was highest for sleep stage N2 (recall 83.5%) and moderate for sleep stage R (recall 68.7%), while Wake and N1 recall were low. Despite lower agreement with the reference than contemporary deep learning models, the method provides deterministic decisions and natural language explanations aligned with AASM scoring rules, making it a complementary tool for auditing, debugging, and governing deep learning-based sleep staging.
2605.22859.paper_summary_zh: 自動化睡眠分期通常被視為一個監督式機器學習問題，深度學習方法在最近的研究中佔據主導地位。雖然機器學習模型在與人類評分的參考睡眠階段達成近似人類水平的一致性，但它們的決策通常是不透明的，並且未設計為遵循臨床評分規則。我們提出了一個透明的替代方案：一種確定性、基於規則的睡眠分期方法，明確地將美國睡眠醫學學會（AASM）的評分邏輯具體化為可執行的代碼，並結合從解釋追蹤中得出的逐時自然語言解釋。我們在50個多導睡眠圖記錄上評估了這種方法，並以10位評分者的多數投票共識作為參考。在所有記錄中，該方法在60.5%的時期內與多數投票參考達成一致（$κ=0.42$），而在開發期間使用的數據集上達成的協議顯著更高（77.1%，$κ=0.61$）。對於睡眠階段N2，與參考的一致性最高（召回率83.5%），對於睡眠階段R則為中等（召回率68.7%），而清醒和N1的召回率較低。儘管與參考的一致性低於當代深度學習模型，但該方法提供確定性的決策和與AASM評分規則一致的自然語言解釋，使其成為審核、調試和管理基於深度學習的睡眠分期的補充工具。
2605.22859.author: Emil Hardarson et.al.
2605.22859.authors: Emil Hardarson, Konstantin Popov, Sigridur Sigurdardottir, Anna Sigridur Islind, Erna Sif Arnardóttir, María Óskarsdóttir
